Technology.thesis: The AI capex cycle continues to be the dominant driver, with strong demand for semiconductors, AI servers, and cloud infrastructure. Software companies are also seeing tailwinds from AI integration, though some are facing valuation and guidance challenges.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, hurting growth stock multiples
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging — significant supply chain disruption

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Consumer spending remains a key concern, with some retailers showing resilience while others face challenges. Auto manufacturers are benefiting from EV adoption, and residential construction is seeing some positive signals.
watchFor: Consumer Confidence Index drops below 95 — signals broader spending slowdown
Retail sales (ex-auto) show negative MoM growth — indicates consumer weakness
Mortgage rates break above 7.5% — would cool housing market activity

Communication Services.thesis: The sector is seeing a mix of AI-driven opportunities and content-related challenges. Social media platforms are exploring new monetization avenues, while streaming services face intense competition.
watchFor: Meta reports slowing ad revenue growth despite AI investments
Netflix subscriber growth disappoints for two consecutive quarters
Regulatory scrutiny on social media platforms increases significantly

Industrials.thesis: Defense spending remains a strong tailwind for aerospace and defense companies, driven by geopolitical factors and new contract wins. Logistics firms are seeing mixed signals, while space-related industrials are gaining momentum.
watchFor: Significant cuts to global defense budgets
Major geopolitical de-escalation events
Global trade volumes decline for two consecutive quarters

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant upside for leaders, while other segments like medical devices face demand slowdowns and healthcare plans navigate regulatory pressures.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy/safety profiles
Regulatory changes impact PBMs or drug pricing significantly
Broader healthcare spending slows due to economic downturn

Financials.thesis: Banks are showing mixed signals, with some anticipating strong trading revenue while others face stock declines. Credit services continue to perform well, and asset managers are seeing positive analyst attention.
watchFor: Fed signals clear dovish pivot (rate cuts) — would boost bank lending but pressure NIMs
Unexpected rise in loan delinquencies — indicates economic stress
Major regulatory changes impacting bank capital requirements

Consumer Defensive.thesis: Discount stores and household product companies are showing resilience amidst varying consumer spending patterns, with some facing valuation questions despite consistent performance.
watchFor: Unexpected surge in inflation, eroding consumer purchasing power
Significant shift in consumer preferences towards premium brands
Strong economic growth leads to trade-up from discount stores

Utilities.thesis: Independent power producers, particularly those focused on clean energy and AI data center power, are seeing increased investor interest and strategic deals.
watchFor: Interest rates rise significantly, increasing cost of capital for energy projects
Regulatory changes hinder renewable energy development
Demand for data center power slows unexpectedly

Basic Materials.thesis: The industrial metals and mining sector is experiencing mixed signals, with some companies facing headwinds despite strategic partnerships, while others benefit from broader market trends.
watchFor: Global industrial production data shows significant contraction
Commodity prices experience a sharp downturn
Increased geopolitical tensions disrupt supply chains

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is gaining focus as a geopolitical hedge and a safe-haven asset amidst global uncertainties, with bullion prices climbing. Silver is also seeing strong price increases, though its investment case is complicated by fees and tax implications.
watchFor: US Dollar Index (DXY) strengthens significantly and sustainably
Real yields rise sharply, increasing the opportunity cost of holding gold
Geopolitical tensions de-escalate, reducing safe-haven demand
